description: Tokenized Deposits in Japan — Executive / Sales deck — 15 content slides + Thank You + 5 Mermaid appendix diagrams

slides.title: Japan's Banking & Payments Landscape
slides.col1:
Market Scale & Shift
• $5T+ total banking assets — one of the world's largest markets
• Historically cash-dominant culture now accelerating toward digital
• Government-led push: CBDC pilot programme underway since 2022
• JBA (Japan Banking Association) driving industry-wide digital innovation
• Over 100 million retail bank accounts — scale ready for tokenization
slides.col2:
Digital Momentum
• BOJ CBDC Phase 2 pilot engaging major city banks
• Payment Services Act reforms enabling e-money and new entrants
• Tokyo Stock Exchange infrastructure primed for digital securities
• Rising corporate demand for T+0 cross-border settlement
• Fintech Act exemptions lowering barriers for digital asset pilots

slides.title: Japan Regulatory Framework
slides.body: Japan has one of Asia-Pacific's most mature digital-asset regulatory stacks. The FSA's layered framework gives licensed banks a clear path to issue tokenized deposits under existing banking rules — without needing a new licence or stablecoin designation.
table_data: Governs fund transfers, e-money, and prepaid instruments — baseline for deposit tokens
Establishes electronic payment instrument rules; banks exempt as deposit issuers
Covers security token offerings; relevant for DvP and settlement interoperability
Licensed banks retain deposit primacy; tokenized deposits remain bank liabilities
KYC, CDD, and transaction monitoring obligations apply to tokenized instruments

slides.title: Tokenized Deposits Explained
slides.subtitle: What they are, how they differ, and why they matter

slides.title: Tokenized Deposits vs Stablecoins vs CBDCs
slides.body: Three digital forms of money — but with fundamentally different issuers, backing, and regulatory treatment.

slides.title: Why Tokenized Deposits Matter
slides.item1:
**Programmable Money**
Smart contract controls enable conditional release, scheduled payments, and automated compliance checks — without manual intervention at each step.
slides.item2:
**24/7 Settlement**
Tokenized deposits settle on-chain at any hour, eliminating cut-off times and end-of-day batch processing that delay corporate treasury cycles.
slides.item3:
**Reduced Counterparty Risk**
DvP atomicity ensures delivery and payment happen simultaneously — eliminating the settlement lag that creates counterparty exposure in traditional rails.
slides.item4:
**Cross-Border Efficiency**
Instant bilateral settlement between Japanese banks and global counterparties replaces multi-day SWIFT chains and correspondent bank queues.

slides.title: DALP for Tokenized Deposits
slides.subtitle: End-to-end lifecycle management, compliance built in

slides.title: DALP: Digital Asset Lifecycle Platform
slides.body:
DALP is SettleMint's production-grade platform for issuing, managing, and servicing tokenized financial instruments.

• EVM-native — deploys on public, private, or consortium chains
• Compliance-first — KYC, AML, and policy controls baked into every action
• Deposit-class support — native module for bank-issued deposit tokens
• API-first architecture — integrates with core banking, SWIFT, and RTGS
• Proven at scale — deployed across regulated institutions in EMEA and Asia

slides.title: Compliance & Policy Controls
slides.body:
Every issuance, transfer, and redemption on DALP passes through a configurable compliance gate before touching on-chain state.

• Whitelist enforcement — only KYC-verified wallets can hold or receive tokens
• Jurisdiction rules — FSA-aligned policies applied per transaction
• Freeze and seizure — regulatory hold and account suspension in one action
• Audit trail — immutable log of every decision, rejection, and approval
• Maker-checker — dual-control for high-value operations
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/11 - Compliance and Policy Templates/Compliance Policy Templates.jpg

slides.title: Deposit Issuance & Lifecycle on DALP
slides.col1:
Mint
• Bank configures deposit token parameters in Asset Designer
• FSA-compliant metadata and token standards set at creation
• KYC and whitelist checks gate every minting action
• Supply cap and issuance velocity rules enforced on-chain
• Maker-checker approval for institutional issuance events
slides.col2:
Transfer & Hold
• On-chain transfer subject to real-time policy engine checks
• Hold instruction freezes specific tokens pending dispute or compliance review
• Partial transfer and fractional ownership fully supported
• Cross-entity and cross-jurisdiction rules applied automatically
• Every event timestamped and stored in immutable audit log
slides.col3:
Redeem & Burn
• Redemption request triggers bank-side fiat return workflow
• DALP burns token supply on confirmed fiat settlement
• Automated reconciliation matches on-chain and off-chain records
• Redemption audit trail available for FSA reporting
• Full lifecycle closed atomically — no manual reconciliation gap

slides.title: Use Cases & Business Value
slides.subtitle: Where tokenized deposits deliver measurable impact

slides.title: Settlement & Interoperability
slides.body: DALP's XVP engine enables atomic delivery-versus-payment across heterogeneous asset classes and settlement rails — connecting deposit tokens directly to Japan's existing securities and payments infrastructure.
table_data: Deposit token ↔ security token swap in one transaction
Cross-vehicle protocol: cash, securities, FX in single atomic event
On-chain settlement replaces T+2 batch settlement windows
ISO 20022 message bridge into DALP for instruction flow
Compatible with ZENGIN and SWIFT gpi
Real-time gross settlement hook for high-value transactions

slides.title: Key Benefits & Business Case
slides.body: Tokenized deposits on DALP deliver measurable operational improvements across settlement, compliance, and treasury operations — with a deployment model that fits Japanese banking infrastructure.

slides.title: Deployment & Integration Options
slides.col1:
Cloud & On-Premise
• Full cloud deployment on AWS, Azure, or GCP
• On-premise or private cloud for air-gapped banking environments
• Hybrid topology: compliance logic on-prem, settlement on-chain
• Managed SaaS with 99.9% SLA for core banking integration
• Data residency controls aligned to Japanese regulatory requirements
slides.col2:
API-First Integration
• RESTful APIs for core banking system connectors
• Webhook-based event streaming for real-time reconciliation
• SDK for Japanese banking middleware (NTT Data, Hitachi, Fujitsu)
• Pre-built connectors for ISO 20022 messaging standards
• Sandbox environment for pre-production integration testing
slides.col3:
Japan Market Fit
• BOJ-NET RTGS integration layer for high-value settlement
• Tokyo Stock Exchange digital securities infrastructure alignment
• ZENGIN payment network bridge for retail-side flows
• JBA Digital Innovation framework compliance mapping
• Localised support team and Japanese-language documentation

slides.title: Implementation Roadmap
slides.body:
A phased approach reduces regulatory risk and ensures production readiness before full launch.

**Phase 1 — Discovery (Months 1–2)**
• Map use cases to Japan's regulatory framework
• Define token parameters, compliance rules, and integration scope
• Stakeholder alignment across legal, risk, and technology teams

**Phase 2 — Pilot (Months 3–5)**
• DALP environment provisioned and configured for deposit asset class
• KYC/AML integration with existing identity infrastructure
• Controlled pilot issuance with select counterparties
• FSA regulatory review and sandbox engagement

**Phase 3 — Production (Months 6–9)**
• Full compliance sign-off and audit trail validated
• Core banking integration completed and load-tested
• Live production launch with BOJ-NET and SWIFT connectivity

slides.title: Why SettleMint
slides.item1:
**Regulated-First Platform**
DALP was built for regulated financial instruments from the start — not retrofitted. Compliance, audit trails, and policy controls are architectural, not optional modules.
slides.item2:
**Global Deployments**
Live in production across banks, exchanges, and custodians in Europe, Middle East, and Asia. Deposit tokenization programmes running at scale in multiple jurisdictions.
slides.item3:
**EVM Expertise**
Deep EVM protocol knowledge: ERC-3643 compliant tokens, multi-chain deployment, and integration with public and private blockchain infrastructure.
slides.item4:
**Enterprise Support & SLAs**
24/7 technical support, 99.9% uptime SLA, dedicated solution engineering for Japan market onboarding, and FSA regulatory engagement support.

slides.title: Appendix
slides.subtitle: Reference diagrams: architecture, settlement flow, regulatory mapping, and roadmap

slides.title: Diagram 1: Tokenized Deposit Lifecycle
slides.body: End-to-end flow from bank origination through KYC screening, on-chain minting, lifecycle controls (transfer, hold, freeze), redemption, and token burn. Each transition is governed by DALP's policy engine.

slides.title: Diagram 2: DALP Platform Architecture
slides.body: DALP's five-layer architecture for tokenized deposits: Asset Designer configures parameters, the Compliance Engine enforces KYC and policy rules, the Token Ledger holds EVM smart contracts, Data Feeds connect fiat oracles and reconciliation, and the Reporting Layer delivers FSA-ready audit output.

slides.title: Diagram 3: DvP Settlement with XVP
slides.body: Atomic T+0 settlement sequence between Buyer, DALP, Custodian, and Counterparty. DALP's XVP engine locks both legs of the trade in escrow before executing the atomic swap — eliminating settlement risk entirely.

slides.title: Diagram 4: Japan Regulatory Mapping
slides.body: FSA regulatory hierarchy mapped to DALP's compliance layer. The Payment Services Act, Stablecoin Act, and FIEA each impose specific obligations — DALP's policy engine and audit infrastructure map directly to these requirements.

slides.title: Diagram 5: Implementation Roadmap
slides.body: Three-phase delivery plan from Discovery through Pilot to Production over 9 months. Phase 1 covers regulatory mapping and use-case definition. Phase 2 runs a controlled pilot. Phase 3 achieves live production with full FSA compliance and core banking integration.
